name: Sierra del Moncayo — circular (PR-Z 182)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ambel offers routes through a landscape characterized by the Sierra del Moncayo mountain range, featuring significant elevation changes and diverse terrain. The region includes natural parks and areas with prominent peaks and hills, providing varied challenges for mountain bikers. Trails often navigate through forested areas and open vistas, with some routes passing by reservoirs. The topography supports both demanding climbs and more moderate paths. Best mountain bike trails around Ambel The most popular mountain bike trail is Vistas a La Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón y las Peñas de Herrera — circular desde Añón de Moncayo, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón, and Peñas de Herrera. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tabuenca - Talamantes - Trasobares - Tabuenca — circular Parque Natural del Moncayo, a difficult 32.1 miles (51.7 km) path. This route traverses the diverse landscapes of the Moncayo Natural Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra del Moncayo — circular (PR-Z 182), a 25.4 miles (40.9 km) trail leading through the scenic Sierra del Moncayo, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Ambel is defined by mountainous terrain, natural park landscapes, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Ambel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ambel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ambel?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails around Ambel, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 4 easy routes, 26 moderate routes, and 9 difficult trails to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ambel?
A: Mountain biking around Ambel is characterized by mountainous terrain, significant elevation changes, and diverse landscapes within the Sierra del Moncayo and Moncayo Natural Park. Trails often navigate through forested areas, open vistas, and some even pass by reservoirs, offering a varied riding experience.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ambel?
A: Yes, Ambel offers 4 easy mountain bike trails. These routes provide a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes, allowing beginners to enjoy the scenery without overly challenging technical sections or steep climbs.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Ambel?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ambel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging mountainous terrain, the diverse landscapes of the natural park, and the significant elevation changes that make for engaging rides.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ambel?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Ambel are circular. For example, you could try the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986762639/vistas-a-la-muela-de-horcajuelo-cerro-morron-y-las-penas-de-herrera-circular-desde-anon-de-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vistas a La Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón y las Peñas de Herrera — circular desde Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views, or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986733015/sierra-del-moncayo-circular-pr-z-182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Moncayo — circular (PR-Z 182)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Ambel?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Ambel offers 9 difficult mountain bike trails. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984484677/tabuenca-talamantes-trasobares-tabuenca-circular-parque-natural-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabuenca - Talamantes - Trasobares - Tabuenca — circular Parque Natural del Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.7 km path traversing the Moncayo Natural Park, or the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enbalse alto de El Prado loop from Vera de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 65.5 km with significant elevation.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking around Ambel?
A: While mountain biking around Ambel, you can encounter several natural features and landmarks. These include viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Peñas de Herrera&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Morrón Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2964121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Boticario Spring&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the mountain bike trails in Ambel?
A: Yes, you can combine your mountain biking with visits to cultural sites. The historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borja&lt;/a&gt; is nearby, and you might also consider visiting the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building in the region.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Moncayo Natural Park?
A: Absolutely. The Moncayo Natural Park is a prominent feature of the region. A great option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984484677/tabuenca-talamantes-trasobares-tabuenca-parque-natural-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabuenca - Talamantes - Trasobares - Tabuenca — circular Parque Natural del Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses the diverse landscapes of the park, offering stunning views.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Ambel?
A: One of the longest mountain bike trails in Ambel is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enbalse alto de El Prado loop from Vera de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans approximately 65.5 kilometers and typically takes around 6 hours and 39 minutes to complete, featuring substantial elevation gain.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by reservoirs in Ambel?
A: Yes, some mountain bike routes in the Ambel area navigate through landscapes that include reservoirs. While specific routes are not detailed, the region's topography and natural features suggest that you may encounter these on certain trails, particularly those in the Moncayo Natural Park area.
tips: Also called Palacio de los Hospitalarios, it is a fortress located in the municipality of Ambel. On December 4, 2001, it was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest.

The Talamantes castle was built in the 11th century with the purpose of defending the border crossing between Castile and Aragon in the Valdeherrera and Valdetreviño ravines. Although much of the fortification is in a state of ruin, the north wall and one of its towers are still quite well preserved.
